briefing.opening: Hello everyone. Welcome to the climate briefing for May 28, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Escalating Global Climate Crisis Warnings and Severe Impacts
sections.content: Globally, the alarm bells of the climate crisis are ringing louder. In Europe, despite it being May, unprecedented and record-breaking heatwaves have struck regions including the UK and France. The UN Secretary-General has characterized this as a 'harsh warning' of the climate crisis and urged a shift away from fossil fuel dependence [1, 3, 12, 13, 14, 126, 128, 147]. These extreme weather phenomena demonstrate a continuous rise in global temperatures [17, 145], manifesting in various forms such as an increase in larger and more destructive hailstorms [16, 131, 139] and a rapid decrease in the Dead Sea's water level [9]. Furthermore, climate change is increasing antibiotic resistance in Salmonella, posing a serious threat to the health sector [2, 6, 10], and is having widespread impacts across all aspects of human life, including a surge in malaria cases in Zimbabwe [125].

sections.title: 🇰🇷🌍 Climate Crisis Response Policies and Implementation: Challenges and Progress
sections.content: While policy efforts to address the era of climate crisis continue, challenges also persist. Lee Mi-sun, head of the Korea Meteorological Administration, announced plans to strengthen responses to climate disasters by forecasting risks with greater emphasis in this era of climate crisis, and by establishing new 'Severe Heatwave Warnings' and 'Tropical Night Advisories' [4, 18, 123]. Internationally, the UN is initiating follow-up measures on the International Court of Justice (ICJ) advisory opinion regarding climate change, seeking global cooperation [127]. Domestically, however, the National Assembly's Special Committee on Climate Change concluded its term without processing amendments to the Carbon Neutrality Framework Act, drawing strong criticism [19, 20, 23, 46]. Concerns are rising that this could lead to policy confusion and even delay companies' green transition efforts [20]. This situation aligns with the international community's call for politicians to no longer ignore climate change warnings [136].

sections.title: 🌱🇰🇷 Expansion of Domestic Carbon Neutrality Initiatives and Exploration of Growth Strategies
sections.content: Domestically, various efforts to transition to a carbon-neutral society are bearing fruit. Pohang City is promoting its transformation from a steel city to a carbon-neutral industrial city and is conducting a final review of its strategy to host COP [7, 26]. Market interest is also growing, with carbon credit prices recovering to the 20,000 won range for the first time in 3 years and 7 months [15]. Arguments have been made that carbon neutrality should be recognized not merely as a cost, but as a new growth strategy [36]. Local governments and businesses are engaging in concrete actions, including projects to strengthen climate disaster response [11], carbon neutrality practice campaigns commemorating Environment Day [21, 45], carbon neutrality cooperation at rest areas [24], and the expansion of blue carbon initiatives [30, 39, 50]. Notably, Hyosung Heavy Industries' Changwon plant is strengthening its low-carbon production system through the expansion of renewable energy power [42, 71, 72, 73, 75, 78, 81, 89, 93, 94, 95, 99, 100, 101, 102, 106, 108, 110, 111, 116, 117, 119], and the government is officially recommending decarbonization to the medical sector, encouraging society-wide carbon neutrality efforts [29, 124].

sections.title: 🌱⚠️ Accelerated Renewable Energy Transition and Emerging Challenges
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is accelerating, but challenges that need to be addressed are also emerging. Korea Southern Power (KOSPO) has unveiled a large-scale investment roadmap, aiming to expand renewable energy facilities to 11.2 GW by 2040, a tenfold increase from current levels, and to supply a cumulative 3.4 GW over the next five years. KOSPO has embarked on building a renewable energy alliance centered on offshore wind power and Energy Storage Systems (ESS) [51, 52, 54, 56, 58, 59, 61, 64, 66, 67, 68, 69, 76, 77, 85, 86, 88, 91, 92, 96, 97, 105, 109]. Domestic companies are also actively expanding overseas, with Hyundai Engineering, for instance, breaking ground on a solar power plant in Texas, USA, marking its full-fledged entry into the North American renewable energy market [74, 83, 90, 98, 103, 104, 112, 113, 115, 118, 120, 122]. However, concerns about conflicts with development projects are also being raised, including worker safety issues during the expansion of renewable energy [60] and growing warnings about the proliferation of 'Sunlight Income Villages' [62]. The Minister of Climate emphasized the necessity of concurrently utilizing nuclear power and renewable energy amidst persistent pressure for electricity bill increases [107, 121], suggesting that meticulous policy design is crucial for a stable energy transition.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has clearly illustrated the severity of the global climate crisis, the diverse domestic and international efforts to address it, and the challenges encountered in this process. For a sustainable future, international cooperation and the consistent implementation of domestic policies will become even more crucial. This concludes today's climate briefing.
